intro.uz: Tom Peters kitobidan olingan gʻoya: kuchli rahbarlar xato qilishdan qoʻrqmaydi — ular xato qiladi, oʻrganadi va yana urinib koʻradi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatik mavzusi shu gʻoyadan oʻsib chiqadi: hayotiy tajriba uchun present perfect (Have you ever…? / ever / never) va oʻtmishga afsus bilan qarash (should have / could have + needn't have). Todd va Louis pingvinlar haqidagi POV hikoyada birinchisini eshitasiz.
intro.en: An idea from Tom Peters: strong leaders are not afraid of mistakes — they make them, learn, and try again. This week's two grammar topics grow from that idea: the present perfect for life experience (Have you ever…? / ever / never) and looking back with regret (should have / could have + needn't have). You'll hear the first one in the POV story about the penguins Todd and Louis.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida “Have you ever …?” savolini bering va unga toʻxtamasdan javob bering, hamda oʻtmishdagi xato haqida afsus bilan gapiring: “I should have …, I could have …, I needn't have … .”
canDo.en: By the end you can ask and answer “Have you ever …?” out loud without pausing, and talk about a past mistake with hindsight: “I should have …, I could have …, I needn't have … .”

grammar.titleUz: Tajriba: Have you ever…? / ever / never
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 — answer “Have you ever…?” about your life experience
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can ask and answer about life experiences (ever / never / before)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekcha “Hech qachon xato qilganmisiz?” gapida oddiy oʻtgan zamonni ishlatamiz. Ingliz tili esa aniq vaqt aytilmagan hayotiy tajriba uchun present perfect talab qiladi: “Have you ever made a mistake?”. Aniq vaqt bor boʻlsa (yesterday, in 2019) — past simple; aniq vaqt yoʻq, faqat “hayotda boʻlganmi?” boʻlsa — present perfect.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ Did you ever go to London? / I have never not failed. ✅ Have you ever been to London? I have never failed. Aniq vaqtsiz tajriba uchun have/has + V3; “never” oʻzi inkor beradi, yana “not” qoʻshilmaydi.

examples.en: Have you ever made a big mistake?
Todd and Louis have never given up.
examples.uz: Todd va Louis hech qachon taslim boʻlmagan.

exercises.prompt: ___ you ever failed at something big?
exercises.hintUz: aniq vaqtsiz tajriba savoli → Have + you + V3

exercises.prompt: Have you ever ___ a new language?

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “I have never … , but I have … .”
exercises.hintUz: have/has + V3; ever savolda, never inkorda

grammar.titleUz: should have / could have (+ needn't have) — afsus
grammar.titleEn: should have / could have — regret & hindsight
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 2/3 — reflect on a past choice: what you should/could have done
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can express regret and hindsight about past actions (should/could/needn't have + V3)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekcha “qilishim kerak edi” bitta ibora. Ingliz tili “hozir kerak” (I should try) va “oʻtmishda kerak edi” (I should have tried) ni have + V3 bilan ajratadi. should have = qilishim kerak edi (qilmadim); could have = qila olardim (imkon bor edi); needn't have = shart emas edi (lekin qildim).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I should of tried. / I could tried harder. ✅ I should have tried. I could have tried harder. Nutqda “should've” eshitiladi, lekin yozuvda doim have + V3 — hech qachon “of” emas.

examples.en: They could have quit, but they kept swimming.
examples.uz: Ular taslim boʻlishlari mumkin edi, lekin suzishda davom etdi.

examples.en: I needn't have worried about the mistake.
examples.uz: Xato haqida qaygʻurishim shart emas edi.

exercises.hintUz: afsus → should have + V3; yozuvda “of” emas, “have”

exercises.prompt: She ___ asked for help, but she didn't.
exercises.hintUz: boy berilgan imkon → could have + V3

exercises.prompt: You ___ worried so much — it was fine!
exercises.hintUz: behuda qilingan ish → needn't have + V3

exercises.promptUz: Oʻz xatoingiz haqida rost gap ayting: “I should have … , I could have … , but I needn't have … .”

vocab.uz: biror narsani yashirmaslik, uzr soʻramaslik; ochiq aytmoq
vocab.defEn: to say or do something openly, without apologizing or hiding it
vocab.example: Leaders make mistakes and they make no bones about it.
In placid times, leaders can pretend to have all the answers.

vocab.uz: notinch, notoʻxtov oʻzgaruvchan, notinch/dovulli (placid ning teskarisi)
vocab.defEn: not calm; rough, chaotic, changing fast
vocab.example: In turbulent times, leaders need the best questions.

vocab.uz: boshlamoq, qoʻl urmoq (uzoq davom etadigan ishga)
vocab.defEn: to start something, usually something long or difficult
vocab.example: Against long odds, they undertook the trip to Thailand.

vocab.uz: birgalikda yangilik oʻrganish sayohati (mutual = umumiy, birgalikdagi)
vocab.defEn: a shared journey of learning new things together
vocab.example: Leaders send their people on voyages of mutual discovery.

vocab.uz: mohiyati, asosiy magʻzi, eng muhim gʻoyasi
vocab.defEn: the core or most important part of something
vocab.example: The essence of trying new things is letting people screw up.

vocab.defEn: to make a mistake or fail (informal)
vocab.example: If you try new stuff, you screw up.

vocab.defEn: required and necessary; something you must do
vocab.example: Mistakes are not enough — big mistakes are mandatory.

vocab.defEn: average; not good and not bad, just so-so
vocab.example: Reward excellent failures, punish mediocre successes.

vocab.uz: beparvo, xavf-xatarga eʼtibor bermay ish tutadigan
vocab.defEn: taking big risks without caring about the danger
vocab.example: They were reckless, but not thoughtless.

vocab.uz: muvaffaqiyat ehtimoli past boʻlsa ham, qiyinchiliklarga qaramay
vocab.defEn: with a very small chance of success; against difficult challenges
vocab.example: Against long odds, they tried to create a new world.

englishpod.title: The Office – Interview Skills: Talking About Weaknesses
englishpod.titleUz: Ofis — suhbat koʻnikmalari: kamchiliklar haqida gapirish

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda Rebecca ish suhbatida oʻz kamchiligini aytishi kerak. AJ ning darsiga juda mos: kuchli odam xato va kamchiliklarini yashirmaydi — ochiq aytadi. Sizningcha, ish suhbatida oʻz kamchiligingizni aytish kuchmi yoki zaiflik?
warmup.en: In this dialogue Rebecca has to describe her weakness in a job interview. It fits AJ's lesson perfectly: strong people don't hide their mistakes and weaknesses — they make no bones about them. Do you think naming your weakness in an interview is a strength or a weakness?

keyVocab.defEn: to locate or describe something exactly or precisely
the feeling or opinion one has after a specific event
upset or annoyed because you cannot do or get what you want
to proceed according to one's plans or wishes
to exhaust, discourage, or depress someone
to avoid or neglect a duty or responsibility

quiz.q: Who said 'honesty is the best policy'?
quiz.qUz: “Halollik — eng yaxshi siyosat” degan gapni kim aytgan?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: b) Benjamin Franklin. U buni 1732–1758-yillar orasida yozilgan “Poor Richard's Almanac” maqollar kitobida yozgan. Rob ham shu javobni tanladi va toʻgʻri topdi.

vocab.defEn: truthful and not trying to cheat people
to behave dishonestly to get what you want
a person who buys goods or services for their own use
the way the human mind works; the way we think
caring only about yourself and not about other people
willing to work together with others towards a shared goal

funEnglish.title: The power of mistakes and failure | Suzi LeVine | TEDxBern

speakingPrompt.en: Have you ever made a big mistake or failed at something? What happened — and looking back, what should you have done differently? What could you have done, and what needn't you have worried about?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: present perfect tajriba uchun (Have you ever …? / I have never …) va oʻtmishga afsus (I should have …, I could have …, I needn't have …).
